channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 'End of Days' recalls the violent 1992 Ruby Ridge confrontation in Idaho
item.description: Author Chris Jennings talks the apocalyptic religious views that fueled the standoff between federal agents and the family of Randy Weaver — and the u...

item.title: Immigration courts fast-track hearings for Somali asylum claims
item.description: Their lawyers fear the notices are merely the first step toward the removal without due process of Somali asylum applicants in the country.

item.title: Japan's Takaichi to pursue conservative agenda after election landslide
item.description: Japan's first female Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, brought the ruling Liberal Democratic Party its biggest-ever electoral victory, fueling her ambit...

item.title: Ilia Malinin's Olympic backflip made history. But he's not the first to do it
item.description: U.S. figure skating phenom Ilia Malinin did a backflip in his Olympic debut, and another the next day. The controversial move was banned from competit...

item.title: Nancy Guthrie search enters its second week as a purported deadline looms
item.description: "This is very valuable to us, and we will pay," Savannah Guthrie said in a new video message, seeking to communicate with people who say they're holdi...

item.title: The W2 Employee’s Roadmap to Financial Freedom (Buy Rentals While Working 8-6)
item.description: Think you’re too busy to own rental properties? Real estate investing doesn’t have to dominate your time or energy. Today’s guest is living proof, hav...

item.title: Databricks CEO says SaaS isn’t dead, but AI will soon make it irrelevant
item.description: AI isn't going to replace major SaaS apps with vibe-coded versions, Databricks Ali Ghodsi believes. But it could give rise to competitors.

item.title: Bluesky finally adds Drafts
item.description: The move comes as Bluesky’s competitors, X and Threads, have long supported drafts.

item.title: MrBeast’s company buys Gen Z-focused fintech app Step
item.description: YouTube megastar MrBeast announced on Monday that his company, Beast Industries, is buying Step, a teen-focused banking app.

item.title: Anthropic’s India expansion collides with a local company that already had the name
item.description: India's Anthropic Software has taken the U.S. AI giant to court over a name dispute.

item.title: Ghislaine Maxwell appeals for clemency from Trump as she declines to answer questions from lawmakers
item.description: House lawmakers have tried to interview Ghislaine Maxwell, but the former girlfriend and confidant of Jeffrey Epstein invoked her Fifth Amendment righ...

item.title: Savannah Guthrie makes new video plea as missing mother’s ransom deadline nears
The Small Sacrifices That Gave Me 25 Rentals and $18,000/Month Cash Flow
item.description: One property can change your entire life. Less than a decade after buying your first, you could be completely financially free, like today’s guest, wh...

item.title: Masks emerge as symbol of Trump’s ICE crackdown and a flashpoint in Congress
ChatGPT rolls out ads
item.description: OpenAI faced a backlash late last year when it tested app suggestions that looked like unwanted ads. Still, the AI company needs to generate revenue f...

item.title: California Governor Gavin Newsom Calls For the State to Implement Its Own Institutional Investor Ban
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom has rarely been in lockstep with the federal government recently, but they agree on one issue: stopping corporate inv...

item.title: The 7 Places Self-Managing Landlords Lose Money Without Realizing It
item.description: This article is presented by RentRedi. When I bought my first rental, I was determined to self-manage. Not because I loved property management, but be...

item.title: A Fast-Growing Renter Demographic is Creating Better Cash Flow Opportunities For Investors
item.description: Solo living is no longer a state enforced on a spouse when their other half passes away, but increasingly a lifestyle choice many Americans prefer or ...
